title: nephrolepis flexuosa
intro: Nephrolepis flexuosa, also known as the Fishbone Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its long, narrow fronds that resemble fishbone patterns. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Nephrolepis flexuosa include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a topical application, and it is also dried and used in teas or capsules for its medicinal properties.
